summary: Backend-only test of Employer Access Rights gating on salary endpoints (Kankani cmp_527fecdd7c). All 16 pytest cases PASSED. Verified: (A) POST /api/admin/salary-runs, /api/admin/arrear-salary-runs, /api/admin/compliance-salary-runs return 403 for company_admin when firm employer_permissions=[] (no keys); (B) GET /api/admin/salary-runs and /api/admin/arrear-salary-runs also return 403 without salary_process:read; (C) After granting salary_process:read/write + compliance_salary:read/write, company_admin succeeds on GET/POST salary-runs, GET single run, and passes the gate on arrear + compliance POST (no 403); (D) Super admin unaffected regardless of firm perms state (GET/POST all succeed); (E) Run-scoped GET /api/admin/salary-runs/{run_id} works for company_admin with read granted. All 403 responses include an 'Employer Access Rights' hint referencing the missing permission key. Cleanup verified: employer_permissions field restored to UNSET (original state), zero leftover salary/arrear/compliance runs, zero leftover test sessions.

minor.issue: Inconsistency in 'empty array' semantics. GET response comment/field says 'empty array == all_features_enabled' (all_features_enabled: not perms). But require_employer_permission (server.py:2019-2027) treats empty/missing employer_permissions the SAME — set(empty) → key not in → 403 for company_admin. So the 'all_features_enabled' flag in the GET response is misleading when the field is explicitly [] vs unset — both yield the same denial behavior. Not a functional bug for this iteration (denial is the desired behavior), but the comment above EMPLOYER_PERMISSION_KEYS (server.py:1929) says 'Default (missing / empty list) → all features enabled (backward compat)' which contradicts actual runtime behavior. Consider aligning: either (a) treat unset/empty as all-enabled inside require_employer_permission, or (b) update comments/GET response to say 'empty == all denied, only unset field means all enabled'.

action_items: Optional: clarify docstring at server.py:1928-1930 and GET /access-rights response field 'all_features_enabled' — current runtime treats empty and unset identically (both deny company_admin), which contradicts the 'empty means all enabled' comment. Either update the docs/response or make require_employer_permission short-circuit on missing/empty perms to match the documented backward-compat behavior.
critical_code_review_comments: require_employer_permission correctly gates only company_admin — super_admin and sub_admin bypass the firm-level opt-in. Confirmed by TestSuperAdminUnaffected.
arrear_salary.py uses the same helper with salary_process:read (list/get/exports) and salary_process:write (create), matching the review request expectation.
compliance-salary-runs use compliance_salary:read/write consistently across ~15 endpoints (server.py:13990-14398).
PATCH /admin/companies/{company_id}/access-rights validates keys against EMPLOYER_PERMISSION_KEYS whitelist before persisting — good input hygiene.
seed_data_creation: No permanent seed. During the run: (1) temporarily set Kankani employer_permissions to [] and then to [salary_process:read/write, compliance_salary:read/write], restored to UNSET at teardown; (2) created 2 salary_runs (2026-05) that were deleted after the run; (3) injected 1 test session for user_0a38839e3568 (deleted at teardown). Post-run DB verification: field UNSET, zero leftover runs, zero leftover sessions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Kankani admin PIN (1234 in old notes) does NOT work — admin-pin-login requires 6-digit PIN and the user changed it. Workaround: inject a session doc into user_sessions collection for user_0a38839e3568 (see test file's _inject_company_admin_session_async). Super admin login works via POST /api/auth/admin-password-login {email: sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com, password: sharma123}. Employer-permissions admin endpoint is PATCH/GET /api/admin/companies/{cid}/access-rights (NOT /employer-permissions as hinted in the request). Kankani employer_permissions must be restored to UNSET at end (pass permissions=null in PATCH body).
rca_of_issue: No bugs found. The Employer Access Rights gating on salary endpoints works exactly as specified: company_admin blocked without explicit firm-level grant, super/sub_admin unaffected, granted company_admin proceeds to normal computation without permission block.
